summary: The Arab world's most populous country, guardian of the Suez Canal (through which 12% of global trade passes), and a US-backed military state that controls one of the world's most strategically critical waterways.

history.detail: World's first nation-state; civilisation for 3,000 years.
Transformative for global trade; Britain buys controlling stake 1875.
Military overthrows monarchy; Nasser becomes Arab nationalist icon.
Nasser nationalises canal; UK/France/Israel invade; US forces withdrawal.
Egypt loses Sinai in 6 days; humiliating military defeat.
Sadat signs Camp David; first Arab-Israeli peace; Sadat assassinated 1981.
18-day Tahrir Square protests; Mubarak resigns after 30 years.
General el-Sisi overthrows elected Muslim Brotherhood president Morsi.

finance.currency_role: Egyptian Pound (EGP) — devalued ~70% in 2022-23; IMF dependency

resources.oil_gas: Modest oil; significant Mediterranean gas (Zohr field)
resources.water: Nile River — existential dependency; Ethiopia's GERD dam threatens flow
resources.agriculture: Nile Delta farming; cotton, wheat, citrus

follow_the_money.detail: $9.4B/yr in transit fees; critical global leverage point
$1.3B/yr since Camp David; conditions rarely enforced despite human rights abuses
Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar pumped $20B+ to stabilise Egypt post-2013

theories_disputed.detail: US officially never called it a 'coup' (would legally require cutting aid); widely described as a coup by analysts.

connections.detail: $1.3B/yr military aid; Camp David legacy; Suez access
Cold peace; gas pipeline; security coordination on Gaza
Gulf states major financial backers of Sisi government
